title: Headless Role Invocation (pi Executor)
summary: Package internal/agent executes a role as a subprocess with correct context. Invokes pi -p --append-system-prompt @<promptfile> --tools <allowlist> "<user-prompt>" in the respective worktree. Sets GOLEMIC_RUN_ID, GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG, role-specific GH_TOKEN, and PATH with golemic binary prepended. Enforces timeout from config, kills process group on timeout, captures stdout/stderr to transcript files, and returns exit code (unparsed, for logging only).

stakeholder_intent.goal: Execute a role (dev or reviewer) as a headless subprocess with correct environment and timeout
stakeholder_intent.business_value: Deterministic, isolated agent execution: the runner controls the full environment, timeout, and transcript capture without parsing agent output
stakeholder_intent.trigger: Runner has rendered prompts (I1.5) and created the worktree (I1.4 / I1.8), and is ready to invoke the agent
stakeholder_intent.success_outcome: The agent process runs to completion (or timeout), stdout/stderr are captured to transcript files, and the exit code is returned for logging

in_scope: CWD set to the role's worktree
Environment: GOLEMIC_RUN_ID, GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG, role-specific GH_TOKEN, PATH with golemic binary prepended, model from config
Timeout from config: kill process group on expiry, result mapped to timeout outcome
Exit code returned as informational (not parsed for semantic content)

preconditions: Config is loaded (I0.2) for model selection and timeout
Event log (I1.1) is available for env var
golemic binary is built and available in PATH

business_rules.rule: The pi CLI is invoked with -p (headless/pipe mode), --append-system-prompt @<systemPromptFile>, --tools <allowlist>, and the user prompt as the last positional argument
The process environment includes GOLEMIC_RUN_ID, GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG, role-specific GH_TOKEN, and PATH with the golemic binary directory prepended
business_rules.outcome: Child process sees all four env vars set correctly

business_rules.rule: The model for the role is passed via the --model flag or env var (determined by pi CLI interface)
business_rules.outcome: Config.Models.Dev or Config.Models.Reviewer is used for the respective role

business_rules.rule: Timeout is enforced: if the process exceeds Config.TimeoutMinutes, the entire process group is killed and the result is timeout
business_rules.outcome: Process group is killed via SIGKILL; result is timeout; transcript files contain partial output up to the kill

business_rules.rule: stdout and stderr are captured to separate transcript files under runs/<runId>/<role>.stdout.log and .stderr.log
business_rules.outcome: Transcript files are written; content is not parsed or interpreted

business_rules.rule: The exit code of the agent is logged but not used for loop decisions; the event log is the authoritative source
business_rules.outcome: Exit code is returned by the function; all loop decisions are based on events in the log

business_rules.rule: Tool allowlist for dev is read,bash,write,edit; for reviewer is read,bash (read-only per convention)
business_rules.outcome: Dev gets --tools read,bash,write,edit; reviewer gets --tools read,bash

then.result: Exit code 0 returned; transcript files written
Exit code returned; transcript files written; event log is authoritative for loop decisions
timeout outcome; process group killed; partial transcripts written

interfaces.name: Agent Executor
interfaces.operation: RunRole(ctx, roleConfig) (exitCode int, transcriptPaths TranscriptPaths, err error)

inputs.name: roleConfig
inputs.type: RoleConfig{role, systemPromptFile, userPrompt, worktreeDir, runID, eventLogPath, ghToken, golemicBinaryPath, model, timeout, toolAllowlist}
constraints: worktreeDir must be an existing directory

outputs.body: exitCode = 0, error wraps ErrTimeout, transcript files contain partial output

integration_contracts.request_contract: Full command line with all arguments
integration_contracts.idempotency: Not idempotent (agent may create side effects like git commits)
integration_contracts.retry_policy: No retry; timeout is a final outcome
integration_contracts.failure_mapping: Non-zero exit → exit code returned to caller; timeout → ErrTimeout

security.authentication: GH_TOKEN is set to the role-specific token; the token is never logged or exposed in transcript files
authorization_rules: Dev token is used for dev role; reviewer token is used for reviewer role
PATH is restricted to include only the golemic binary and the system PATH
security.data_classification: Agent output (may contain code or project data); token values must never appear in transcripts
audit_requirements: Transcripts are stored for debugging; exit code is logged

architecture_constraints: Process group must be killed on timeout (not just the parent process)
stdout/stderr are captured to files, not piped through Go for parsing
The golemic binary path is prepended to PATH so agent subcommands (golemic emit, open-pr, submit-review) are available
forbidden_changes: Do not parse or interpret stdout/stderr for loop decisions
Do not pass GH_TOKEN values in command-line arguments
Do not allow the agent process to outlive the timeout
dependencies: Standard library: os/exec, os, context, time, path/filepath, fmt
implementation_context.consistency_and_concurrency: Single-threaded per role; each role runs sequentially. The process group kill ensures cleanup on timeout.

acceptance_scenarios.title: Agent process is called with correct environment
given: A fake executor that records the command and environment
Valid role config for dev role
then: The command includes pi, -p, --append-system-prompt @<file>, --tools read,bash,write,edit, and the user prompt
The environment includes GOLEMIC_RUN_ID, GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG, GH_TOKEN (dev token), and PATH with golemic binary directory prepended

acceptance_scenarios.title: Timeout kills process and returns timeout result
given: A fake process that runs indefinitely

acceptance_scenarios.title: Transcripts are written to correct locations
then: Transcript files are under the runs directory

acceptance_scenarios.title: Reviewer tool allowlist is read-only
given: A valid role config for reviewer role

test_cases: Dev role gets correct environment vars
Reviewer role gets correct environment vars
Timeout kills process and returns timeout error
Transcript files are written at correct paths
Exit code is returned without parsing
Process CWD is set to the worktree directory
non_functional_requirements: Process group must be killed on timeout (not just the parent)
No GH_TOKEN values appear in command-line arguments or transcript paths
definition_of_done: go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test -count=1 ./... is green
Timeout enforcement uses process group kill
Transcript files are written to runs/<runId>/<role>.{stdout,stderr}.log

decision_log.decision: Child process environment includes GOLEMIC_RUN_ID, GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG, role-specific GH_TOKEN, and modified PATH
decision_log.rationale: Specified in §2.6: 'Der Runner setzt beim Headless-Aufruf: GOLEMIC_RUN_ID, GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG, GH_TOKEN, PATH mit vorangestelltem golemic-Binary'

decision_log.decision: Kill the entire process group on timeout, not just the parent process
decision_log.rationale: Specified in backlog: 'Prozessgruppe killen, Ergebnis timeout'
Specified in Iteration 1 description: 'Tool-Allowlists fix: Dev = read,bash,write,edit; Reviewer = read,bash'

decision_log.decision: Agent executor uses os/exec directly (not through the Executor interface) for process spawning
decision_log.rationale: osExecutor in main.go wraps exec.Command for simple Run/RunWithEnv; agent executor needs process group management for timeout, which requires direct os/exec usage

decision_log.decision: Agent reads model name and timeout duration from the Config struct
decision_log.rationale: Config.Models.Dev/Reviewer hold the model names; Config.TimeoutMinutes holds the timeout; both are used by the agent executor

codebase_evidence.finding: Production osExecutor implements the Executor interface with Run and RunWithEnv methods; can be used by agent package for process execution
Config struct has Models.Dev and Models.Reviewer strings and TimeoutMinutes field; agent package will read these for model selection and timeout
